title: Ethiopia, Burundi Deepen Ties As Leaders Oversee Key Cooperation Agreements
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed and President Évariste Ndayishimiye of Burundi have taken a significant step toward strengthening bilateral relations, overseeing the signing of several Memoranda of Understanding (MoUs) during high-level talks in Addis Ababa.

title: Prime Minister Abiy Shares Holiday Gifts With Staff, Orphaned Children
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed shared holiday items this morning with low-income staff members of the Office of the Prime Minister and orphaned children under the office's care.

title: Member of European Parliament Describes Ethiopia's Quest for Sea Access As Logical Right
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- Ethiopia's quest for access to the sea is a logical right as the country is one of the rising states in the middle of various challenges, Thierry Mariani, a Member of the European Parliament (MEP), noted.

title: Burundian Prez Ndayishimiye Arrives in Addis Ababa for Official Working Visit
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- President Évariste Ndayishimiye of Burundi, who is also the current Chairperson of the African Union, arrived in Addis Ababa today for an official working visit.

title: Ethiopia, Burundi Renew Commitment to Deepen Their Cooperation
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- Ethiopia and Burundi have reaffirmed their commitment to strengthening bilateral ties and expanding practical cooperation following the conclusion of the Second Joint Ministerial Commission in Addis Ababa.

title: Ethiopia Secures Landmark Arbitration Wins Worth Over U.S.$850 Million
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- Ethiopia has secured decisive victories in two major international arbitration cases, successfully defending claims exceeding 850 million USD and reinforcing its legal position in global dispute resolution, the Ministry of Justice announced.

title: Ethiopian Airlines Eyes Global Competitiveness As It Marks 80 Years of Service
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- Ethiopian Airlines has reaffirmed its ambition to become one of the world's most competitive carriers as it celebrates its 80th anniversary, aligning its long-term expansion with the Vision 2040 strategy.

title: Djibouti Prioritizes Ethiopian Cargo At Ports, Says Ports Authority Chairman
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- Djibouti will continue to give priority handling to cargo destined for Ethiopia, according to Aboubaker Omar Hadi, Chairman of the Djibouti Ports and Free Zones Authority (DPFZA).

title: New Evidence Suggests Ethiopia Is Supporting the RSF, Sparking Fears of Regional Insecurity
summary: [Ayin Network] New evidence gathered by Yale University's Humanitarian Research Lab (HRL) suggests Ethiopia is supporting the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) through an Ethiopian National Defence Force (ENDF) base in Asosa, Ethiopia.

title: PM Abiy Extends Tenure of Tigray Region Interim Chief Administrator By One Year
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed has extended the appointment of Lieutenant General Tadesse Worede as Chief Administrator of the Tigray Interim Administration for an additional one-year, effective April 9, 2026.

title: Election Board to Deploy Team to Assess Security Situations Ahead of June Election
summary: [Reporter] The National Election Board of Ethiopia (NEBE) prepares to deploy team that will assess the security situation in polling station areas. The board convened with political parties, CSOs and stakeholders on the modality and terms of the assessment. The team will be comprised of the Board, political parties and CSOs, according to NEBE statement today.

title: Ethiopia Unveils Landmark Reform Report Showcasing Strong Growth, Policy Gains
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- The Government of Ethiopia today officially launched the Macroeconomic and Structural Reform Report during a high-level event held at the Commercial Bank of Ethiopia.

title: Ethiopia's Shift in SOEs From Managing Risks to Unlocking Opportunities Maximizing Economic Growth - WB
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- Ethiopia is increasingly shifting its focus from managing risks to unlocking opportunities in the reform of state-owned enterprises (SOEs), aiming to better utilize public assets to drive economic growth and job creation, according to the World Bank Group.

title: Ethiopia Presses Red Sea Access As 'Win-Win' Solution At Strategic Maritime Dialogue
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- Ethiopia's pursuit of sovereign access to the Red Sea is not solely a national ambition but a strategic vision aligned with regional integration and the African Union's Agenda 2063, Vice Admiral Kindu Gezu, Commander-in-Chief of the Ethiopian Navy, said.

title: PM Abiy, Lt. Gen. Tadesse Convene As Tigray Admin Term Nears End
summary: [Reporter] Lieutenant General Tadesse Werede is in Addis Ababa for a meeting with Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed as the life term of the Tigray Regional Administration nears its end.

title: AU Shares Sorrow at Death of Ambassador Konjit Sinegiorgis of Ethiopia
summary: [African Union] The Chairperson of the African Union Commission, H.E. Mahmoud Ali Youssouf, has received with profound sorrow the news of the passing of Ambassador Konjit Sinegiorgis, a distinguished diplomat, a pioneering figure in Ethiopia's foreign service, and a steadfast Pan-Africanist who dedicated her life to the service of her country and the continent.

title: Veteran Diplomat Ambassador Konjit Passes Away
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- Veteran Ethiopian diplomat Konjit Sinegiorgis has passed away while receiving medical treatment, according to the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Ethiopia.

title: PM Abiy Meets Tigray Interim Chief Administrator to Assess Annual Report
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed held talks this morning with Lieutenant General Tadesse Worede, Chief Administrator of the Tigray Region Interim Administration, to review the annual performance report and discuss its key findings.

title: Federal Police Crack Down On Sprawling Human Trafficking Network
summary: [Reporter] - Accused ringleader Yitbarek Dawit detained in Shire

title: From Fiscal Burden to Job Engine - Ethiopia's State-Owned Enterprise Transformation

title: Police Arrest International Human-Trafficker, Nine Accomplices
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- The Ethiopian Federal Police announced today that it has arrested Yitbarek Dawit, a notorious international human-trafficker involved in the death of more than 100 people, raping of over 50 women, and trafficking of more than 3,000 people, along with 9 of his accomplices.

title: Ethiopia Concludes UNHRC Vice-Presidency With Robust Diplomatic Engagement - Foreign Ministry
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- The Ministry of Foreign Affairs affirmed that Ethiopia has concluded its tenure as Vice-President of the United Nations Human Rights Council (UNHRC), described it as a period of active and constructive diplomatic engagement during the Council's 61st regular session in Geneva.

title: Ethiopia, India Expand Cooperation Across Strategic Sectors
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- India and Ethiopia are exploring broader areas of collaboration across a wide range of sectors, according to Indian Ambassador to Ethiopia.

title: Ethiopia to Host Continental Conference On Disaster Risk Capacity
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- Ethiopia is set to host the 16th conference of the African Risk Capacity, bringing together representatives from across the continent to reinforce disaster risk response systems, financing mechanisms and institutional capacity.

title: Ethiopia Among Fastest Growing Tourism Industries Worldwide - Ministry of Tourism
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- The comprehensive reform initiatives undertaken by the government have made Ethiopia one of the fastest growing tourism industries in the world, according to Ministry of Tourism.

title: CSOs Step Up Voter Education, Monitoring Ahead of Ethiopia's 7th General Election
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- Leaders of Ethiopia's major civil society organizations (CSOs) have reaffirmed their commitment to supporting a peaceful, fair, and democratic process in the country's upcoming 7th general election, scheduled for June 1, 2026.

title: From the Horn to the World - Ethiopia's Diplomatic Awakening
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa, April 5, 2026 (ENA) --Over the past eight years, Ethiopia has redefined its diplomatic posture, evolving from a cautious regional actor into an assertive and adaptive player on both continental and global stages. This transformation has been guided by a combination of pragmatic decision-making, economic ambition, and the cultivation of diversified partnerships, reflecting a deliberate effort to secure influence within an increasingly multipolar world.

title: PM Abiy Unveils World-Class Sports Complexes, Signaling Ethiopia's Bold Multi-Sector Rise
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed on Saturday inaugurated a series of world-class sports and recreational facilities in the capital, highlighting the government's broader nation-building agenda aimed at strengthening Ethiopia across all sectors.

title: Ethiopian Horticulture Production Sees Rising Global Demand - Stakeholders
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- Producers and exporters affirmed that Ethiopia's horticulture sector is recording significant growth in the international markets.

title: Ethiopia Assumes Chairship of AU Peace and Security Council
summary: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- Ethiopia has assumed the Chairship of the African Union Peace and Security Council for April 2026, marking its first time leading the Council since rejoining it in April 2025.
